content.verdict: Couples Swept Away occupies a quarter-mile of Negril's legendary Seven Mile Beach — the longest, whitest, palm-lined sand strip in Jamaica and arguably the Caribbean. The resort is adults-only, almost entirely couples-occupied, and built with an unusual emphasis on fitness and active wellness: the 10-acre Sportscomplex across the road has 10 tennis courts, squash, racquetball, lap pool, and full gym facilities all included. For honeymooners who want a long beach, Negril's famous sunsets, and an all-inclusive that isn't food-and-drink-only, this is Couples' strongest property.
content.best_room: All 312 suites at Swept Away face the sea — there are no garden-view rooms, which is unusual in all-inclusives. The Beachfront Verandah Suites are the most popular for couples — direct beach access, large private terraces, and steps to the sand. The Atrium Suites in the higher floors have the best ocean panoramas. The Penthouse Suites add a hot tub on the private terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival on Seven Mile Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Sangster (MBJ) — Couples Swept Away is a 75-minute transfer west to Negril (closer than the north-coast properties). Wristbands on, beachfront welcome cocktail, and the first walk down Seven Mile Beach. Sunset on the beach with the resort's signature rum punch is the obligatory first-night ritual.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Seven Mile Beach & Sunset at Rick's Café
itinerary_7_nights.description: A genuinely lazy beach day — Seven Mile Beach is wide, white, and so long that even at peak season it never feels crowded. Late afternoon trip to Rick's Café on Negril's western cliffs to watch the famous cliff-divers leap from 35 feet into the Caribbean as the sun sets. Back to the resort for dinner at Feathers, the Asian-fusion option.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Negril Cliff Snorkel & Boat Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Couples runs an included boat trip from the resort beach along Negril's famous limestone cliffs — snorkeling stops at the offshore reefs, a swim through the cliff caves, and the dramatic stretch of West End coastline. Lunch back on the resort beach, afternoon at the included scuba program.

itinerary_7_nights.title: YS Falls Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Day excursion 90 minutes east to YS Falls — a series of seven cascading waterfalls in St. Elizabeth Parish, far quieter than Dunn's River. Rope swings into the deep pools, gardens, and a working cattle estate. A genuine inland Jamaica experience away from the cruise-ship circuit. Return for sunset on Seven Mile Beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sportscomplex & Spa Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the 10-acre Sportscomplex — included tennis lesson on one of the 10 courts, lap swim in the Olympic-size pool, and squash for couples who play. Afternoon at the resort spa for a couples' massage in a beachfront cabana. The active-wellness focus is what genuinely distinguishes Swept Away.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Catamaran Cruise & Dunn's River Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Long day east to Dunn's River Falls (90 minutes) — climbing the famous terraced cascade is the essential Jamaica photo. Lunch at Miss T's in Ocho Rios for the best curry goat on the island. Return late afternoon, then the included sunset catamaran cruise from the resort beach with rum and reggae.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Beach Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final long walk down Seven Mile Beach at sunrise — the beach is at its quietest in the morning. Breakfast on the suite terrace, last swim, and transfer back to MBJ. Negril's beach is the sort of place couples book to return to for anniversaries — the loyalty programme tracks repeat visits.

caveats: Negril's Seven Mile Beach is occasionally affected by sargassum seaweed (more in summer/fall) — Couples maintains the beach diligently but check recent reports if travelling June through October.
The resort food is good but typical all-inclusive standard — Feathers (Asian) and Lemongrass (Italian) are the strongest of the seven restaurants; expect hotel cooking, not destination dining.
Negril is far from Kingston, Ocho Rios, and the Blue Mountains — day trips east are long (1.5 to 2.5 hours each way); plan in advance which excursions matter most.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Couples Swept Away is one of the strongest honeymoon options in Negril — adults-only, almost entirely couples, all suites are oceanfront, the Sportscomplex sets it apart from typical all-inclusives, and Seven Mile Beach is genuinely one of the world's great beaches. Honeymooners get complimentary perks (sunset cruise, spa credit on weekly stays) and the loyalty programme rewards return visits.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Mid-December through April is dry season and ideal — sunny days, low humidity, calm seas, no sargassum. May offers excellent value before summer humidity. June through October has hurricane risk and occasional sargassum. November is excellent shoulder.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: High-season honeymoons should book 4 to 6 months ahead, especially for Beachfront Verandah Suites. Atrium Suites have more flexibility. Couples often runs multi-resort split-stay packages — a few nights at Swept Away (Negril) plus Tower Isle or Sans Souci (north coast) gives both beach and reef experiences.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes — strictly adults-only (18+). Guest body is approximately 97% couples. No children on the property at any time. Among the most consistently couples-focused all-inclusives in the Caribbean.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Beachfront Verandah Suites for direct beach access and the easiest beach-to-suite walk. Atrium Suites on higher floors for the best long views down Seven Mile Beach. Penthouse Suites for the private rooftop hot tub if budget allows.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Sangster International (MBJ) in Montego Bay is the gateway — a 75-minute private transfer (included) west to Negril along the coast road. Negril is the closest of Jamaica's main resort areas to MBJ, which is one of Swept Away's quiet advantages over the north-coast properties.
